channel.title: maGaming RSS Feed - insider-gaming.com
channel.description: A cleaned-up version of the original gaming feed for insider-gaming.com

item.title: Esports Nations Cup Will Have Biggest CS2 Qualifier Ever
item.content:encoded:
It has been confirmed that the Esports Nations Cup will have 96 different qualifiers to determine the competing 24 Counter-Strike 2 teams.

With 96 qualifiers across 96 different countries and territories, with more than 15,000 expected players it will be CS2’s largest open qualifier ever.

These National Qualifiers will decide the rosters which will represent each nation, then those teams will compete in Regional Qualifiers.

Unlike most ENC tournaments, there will be no direct invites for the $1.32m CS2 tournament. Teams will have to compete for limited regional spots.

Before going any further, consider downloading the free Insider Gaming app for the cleanest way to read your gaming news on iOS and Android.

Esports Nations Cup CS2 National Qualifiers

The Esports Foundation used the VRS rankings of players to determine which 96 regions were eligible to compete in the qualifiers.

The local qualifiers will take place from July 6-16, and then the regional qualifiers will take place between July 17-19.

Between 32 to 64 rosters will be able to compete to represent their nation at the first-ever ENC, with the usual rules that a roster can only contain a maximum of three players from one professional organisation.

  • Fancy yourself an esports expert? Start betting with a $20 deposit at Polymarket and receive a $50 bonus!

Here are the countries that can host National Qualifiers and, in parentheses, how many slots in the Regional Qualifiers are available:

  • Western Europe (4 ENC Slots): Denmark, France, Sweden, Great Britain, Germany, Spain, Finland, Portugal, Belgium, Norway, the Netherlands, Ireland, Switzerland, Luxembourg, Iceland, and Italy
  • Eastern Europe (4 ENC Slots): Ukraine, Israel, Poland, Lithuania, Romania, Kosovo, Czech Republic, Bulgaria, Hungary, Latvia, Estonia, Slovakia, North Macedonia, Bosnia & Herzegovina, Serbia, and Croatia
  • North America (3 ENC Slots): United States, Canada, Mexico, Guatemala, Panama, Dominican Republic, Costa Rica, and Puerto Rico
  • South America (3 ENC Slots): Brazil, Argentina, Uruguay, Chile, Peru, Colombia, Venezuela, and Paraguay
  • Middle East & Central Asia (3 ENC Slots): Türkiye, Kazakhstan, Uzbekistan, Lebanon, Azerbaijan, Georgia, Iraq, Palestine, United Arab Emirates, Turkmenistan, Kyrgyzstan, Islamic Republic of Iran, Tajikistan, Saudi Arabia, Jordan, and Armenia
  • North Africa (1 ENC Slot): Algeria, Tunisia, Morocco, Egypt
  • Sub Saharan Africa (1 ENC Slot): South Africa, Mauritius, Senegal, Nigeria, Democratic Republic of the Congo, Kenya, Ghana, and Angola
  • South & East Asia (2 ENC Slots): Mongolia, People’s Republic of China, Pakistan, India, Republic of Korea, Hong Kong China, Chinese Taipei, and Japan
  • Southeast Asia (2 ENC Slots): Thailand, Singapore, Indonesia, Vietnam, Malaysia, the Philippines, Cambodia, and Brunei Darussalam
  • Oceania (1 ENC Slot): Australia, New Zealand, Guam, and Fiji

Which players or nations are you most excited for? Let us know on the Insider Gaming Discord.

And for more CS2, check out our interview with Niko after his IEM Cologne triumph.

item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: Steam Machine Was Planned To Be A Lot Cheaper
item.content:encoded:
The Steam Machine has been launched, the prices have been revealed, and Valve has explained its reasoning for the higher costs.

For those who may not be aware, a recent announcement has revealed the long-awaited pricing and ordering details for the Steam Machine. Some users were surprised by the price, with the lowest variant starting at over $1,000. Valve has already provided the reason, explaining that their initial plan was to offer it at a lower price.

Why The Steam Machine Is So Expensive

The blog post announcing the Steam Machine’s pricing also explained the reasoning behind it. Valve stated that their system consists of components sourced from manufacturers around the globe, and the pricing depends on the cost of these components. When the company began developing the Steam Machine in 2023, it believed it understood how component prices would rise over the years. “That understanding was born from the many years of data we all have about the evolution of PC hardware prices – primarily, that it tends to get cheaper over time as new technology arrives.”

  • RELATED: Steam Next Fest is Great, But The Slew of Gen-AI Art and Games Isn’t

However, they could not foresee some of the changes that actually occurred. Valve did not anticipate the shortage and significant rise in prices for RAM and storage components, which are affecting hardware pricing across the board. Hence, they are unable to meet their original pricing goal for the Steam Machine, which was intended to be less expensive than the latest announced prices. The revealed prices “reflect the state of the world for manufacturing; or, more accurately, it reflects the price of the components as we’ve secured them over the past 6 months.”

The post also mentions that both the price and availability of the Steam Machine have been affected by component costs. Due to instances when the company was unable to procure components, the number of units available at launch has also been affected.

What are your thoughts on the Steam Machine’s price? Leave your thoughts down in the comments, and join the official Insider Gaming Discord server.

For more information from Insider Gaming, read about the Sonic Frontier Definitive Edition release date seemingly being revealed. Don’t forget to sign up for our weekly newsletter.

MOBILE APP

item.title: Sonic Frontiers Definitive Edition Release Date Seemingly Revealed by Retailers
item.content:encoded:
It seems that more evidence pointing towards Sonic Frontiers Definitive Edition Switch 2 release date has surfaced online.

Players were already aware that Sonic Frontiers Definitive Edition will be releasing for the Switch 2 on June 23, thanks to a report by a reliable insider last month. Now, it seems there are more details further cementing the suspected release date ahead of the official announcement.

Sonic Frontiers Definitive Edition Physical Copies Spotted in Walmart

Spotted by X user (formerly Twitter) SAAC_News, several users have found physical copies of Sonic Frontiers Definitive Edition for Switch 2 in retail stores, including Walmart. However, when asked whether they could purchase them, the employees said they are not allowed to put them on sale until June 23. This date is also Sonic’s birthday, and 2026 also happens to be the 35th anniversary of the franchise, so what better date to begin the sales?

  • More Insider Gaming News: Sega Almost Ended Sonic Games 10 Years Ago

If you’re curious about what Sonic Frontiers Definitive Edition offers, this new version includes all DLC, updates, and improvements made by the developers since the game’s initial release. Additionally, players can expect enhanced performance and better graphics on the Switch 2, along with reduced load times. This makes it a more technically optimized version compared to the original release on the first Switch.

What are your thoughts on Sonic Frontiers Definitive Edition’s release date being set for June 23? Leave your thoughts down in the comments, and join the official Insider Gaming Discord server.

For more information from Insider Gaming, read about the price of the Steam Machine and details on how to purchase it. Don’t forget to sign up for our weekly newsletter.

item.title: The Steam Machine Will Cost More Than $1,000
item.content:encoded:
The Steam Machine’s price has been revealed, along with what’s included in the package.

Players have been receiving various teasers over the past few months about the Steam Machine, all signaling the new system’s arrival in 2026. Players have now been provided with the price, what’s included, and the different bundle types they can grab when purchasing the new system.

Steam Machine Starting Price Set at $1049

A new blog post from Valve reveals the prices of the Steam Machine across different variants and how players can purchase it. There are four options available for purchase: a 512 GB model, a 512 GB model bundled with a Steam Controller, a 2 TB model, and a 2 TB model bundled with a Steam Controller. Both 2 TB variants feature two additional faceplates: red fabric and solid walnut. Check out the prices for the different Steam Machine storage options and bundles below:

  • Steam Machine 512GB: $1,049 USD/1,509 CAD/1,039 EUR/879 GBP/1,609 AUD/4,389 PLN  
  • Steam Machine 512GB + Steam Controller: $1,128 USD/1,628 CAD/1,108 EUR/938 GBP/1,728 AUD/4,698 PLN
  • Steam Machine 2TB: $1,349 USD/1,919 CAD/ 1,359 EUR/1,149 GBP/2,109 AUD/5,739 PLN
  • Steam Machine 2TB + Steam Controller: $1,428 USD/2,038 CAD/1,428 EUR/1,208 GBP/2,228 AUD/6,048 PLN

How to purchase a Steam Machine?

To order the Steam Machine, players must sign up for the model they are interested in before Thursday, June 25tJune 25a.m. Pacific. On that date, they will conduct a one-time randomization to determine the reservation and waitlist order. Once the process is over, players will receive one of two emails: first, stating that they have been added to the reservation queue and that they will receive a shipment once units are available. The other email states that they have been added to the waitlist and will be notified when more units become available. The first batch of Steam Machine deliveries starts going out on June 29.

What are your thoughts on the Steam Machine’s price and purchase process? Leave your thoughts down in the comments, and join the official Insider Gaming Discord server.

For more information from Insider Gaming, read about Insomniac detailing Wolverine’s adjustable difficulties and skippable puzzles. Don’t forget to sign up for our weekly newsletter.
